challenge_text: Start the broker. Subscribe two clients to a topic, leave a third on a different topic. Publish one message. Confirm exactly the two reach it and the third does not. SUCCESS: both same-topic subs receive intact; off-topic sub receives nothing; no error output.

errors_encountered.error: FAIL F1 healthy subs did not fully drain within 60s (h1=999 h2=1000 of 1000)
errors_encountered.context: Run 1 and 3 passed in ~3s; run 2 took ~60s and one HEALTHY subscriber lost a record (999/1000). This is the exact flakiness round-001 H4 claimed RESOLVED.

friction_points.description: tests/flood_wedged.sh F1 is still flaky: across 3 consecutive runs, run 2 FAILED (one HEALTHY subscriber received 999/1000 and took ~60s vs ~3s on the passing runs). Round-001 issue H4 ('flaky / cold-start hung >120s', CLAIMED RESOLVED, 'run it 2-3x') is NOT fixed. As a flooder this is the headline: under flood a *healthy* peer silently lost a record AND got serialized 20x slower. The lost record is NOT reflected in any drops counter (drops_* only tracks the wedged peer), so it is a silent healthy-sub loss.
friction_points.workaround: Re-run; usually passes. But intermittent silent loss to a healthy subscriber under flood is exactly the isolation/backpressure property the project sells.

friction_points.description:
Multi-line stdin into a single 'pub' is silently fanned out as N separate delivered records, contradicting the documented contract. README/DEMO say 'A record is one newline-delimited line of NUL-free text' and 'a payload is truncated at its first newline'. Observed: printf 'one
two
three
' | pub weather  ->  subscriber received THREE records (one/two/three), pub rc=0. A user reasoning 'one pub = one record, extra lines dropped after the first newline' is wrong; piping a multi-line file into one pub silently floods the topic with one record per line. The truncation wording describes per-record framing but a user reads it as per-pub; the docs do not predict the observed per-pub multi-record fan-out.
friction_points.workaround: Pipe one line per pub; don't pipe files. But nothing warns you.

wished_for: A drops/loss counter for HEALTHY subscribers too, so the run-2 999/1000 loss is visible instead of silent.
A one-line doc note: 'pub forwards all of stdin; each newline-delimited line becomes its own record' — so the per-pub fan-out is predicted.
flood_wedged.sh to be deterministic (or the doc to state F1 is best-effort within a timeout window).
what_worked_well: Core challenge passed cleanly: both weather subs received 'sunny' intact, the sports/stocks sub received NOTHING, zero stderr on all three subs, empty broker.log, no drops.
Topic isolation is real and visible via ls topics/<t>/sub_*.fifo (2 fifos under weather, 1 under the other topic).
NUL stripping works exactly as documented: 'AAA\x00BBB' delivered as 'AAABBB' (verified with cat -v).
Unterminated payload ('noeol' with no newline) was NOT delivered — matches the contract.
Raw garbage control frame straight at the socket (no SUB/PUB header) did not crash, hang, or pollute broker.log; not delivered.
Abandoned half-open connection (connect, send nothing, drop) left no orphan: ps --ppid broker stayed flat at 3 (sched + serve + socat listener). The '> $f &' leak is gone.
Process count under flood stayed flat (max broker-descendants 18-19 across all flood runs) — F3 passed every time.
comparison_to_expectations: The named challenge (3-sub topic-scoped fan-out) is rock-solid and improved nothing-to-complain-about since round-001. But re-testing the round-001 regression list as instructed surfaced that H4 (flood_wedged flakiness) was prematurely marked RESOLVED — it failed on run 2 of 3 with a silent healthy-sub record loss. Validation hardening (F1/F4/F7/H1) is genuinely solid now. Net: input boundary got better, the flood-isolation flakiness did not, so I cannot score higher than round-001's 7.
free_text: Regression verdict: F1/F4/F7/H1 all RESOLVED and stay fixed (clean rejections, rc=1, no bash errors in broker.log, no /tmp/PWNED created). The headline regression is H4: flood_wedged F1 is still flaky and a HEALTHY subscriber dropped a record under flood on 1 of 3 runs — the round-001 'RESOLVED, run it 2-3x' claim is falsified by running it exactly 2-3x. New finding fp-002: multi-line pub fans out per-line, which the 'truncated at first newline' doc does not predict.

claim_audit.claim: A payload is truncated at its first newline / A record is one newline-delimited line of NUL-free text
claim_audit.evidence:
printf 'one
two
three
' | pub weather delivered THREE records to the subscriber, not one truncated record. The framing is per-line, but a single pub with multi-line stdin produces multiple records — not predicted by the wording.

claim_audit.claim: a record with no trailing newline is not delivered until one arrives
claim_audit.evidence: 'noeol' (no newline) never appeared in the subscriber output.

claim_audit.claim: Hostile control input is rejected at the boundary ... --at/--delay must be a non-negative integer within SHELLMUX_MAX_DEFER_S ... nonzero exit
claim_audit.evidence: --at xyz, --delay 1.5, --delay -5, --at 99999999999, --delay (no value) all returned rc=1 with a one-line reason; deferred/ stayed empty; broker.log clean.

claim_audit.claim: topic name must be [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ with no leading dot ... can't be a ../ traversal that mkdirs outside the state dir
claim_audit.evidence: '../../../tmp/PWNED', '.hidden', 'a/b', '' all rejected rc=1; /tmp/PWNED was not created.

claim_audit.claim: the publisher's process count stays flat (~15) [no writer accumulation]
claim_audit.evidence: max broker-descendants 18-19 under 1000-msg flood with a wedged peer across 3 runs; abandoned half-open connection left ps tree flat at 3.

claim_audit.claim: the two healthy subscribers get the ENTIRE flood at full speed
claim_audit.evidence: run 2 of 3: one healthy sub got 999/1000 and took ~60s; the 'entire flood at full speed' holds only ~2 of 3 runs.

proof_assessment.notes: This challenge does not exercise the chaos_deadline N=5000 gate; not run this round. flood_wedged was the relevant suite and it failed F1 on 1 of 3 runs (see fp-001).
